seo.title: Llama Stack Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Llama Stack agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Llama Stack face heightened risks from agentic behaviors like unsubstantiated product claims, inaccurate pricing, and mishandled payment data, which can trigger FTC enforcement, GDPR fines, and PCI DSS violations. Llama Stack's safety shields often produce false positives on benign shopping queries, while memory retrieval hallucinations can misrepresent return policies, eroding customer trust. Vex provides post-processing verification with vex.verify() to ensure agent outputs conform to e-commerce compliance standards before customer exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; agents cannot make unverified health/performance claims about products.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies claims against product catalog and marketing guidelines, blocking unsubstantiated statements with custom policies.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling must include consent verification and data minimization; agents cannot retain or share PII without explicit permission.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent responses for PII leakage and enforces data retention policies via schema validation.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment processing must never expose cardholder data in agent responses or memory; tokenization required.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex redacts PCI-sensitive data and validates payment flows conform to tokenization schemas.

complianceRequirements.description: Product descriptions and checkout flows must be accessible; agents cannot generate non-compliant UI instructions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces WCAG 2.1 compliance checks on generated content and UI recommendations.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: Llama agent fabricates health benefits for supplements without evidence, violating FTC rules.
industryRisks.example: Agent claims 'cures diabetes' for herbal tea based on hallucinated memory retrieval.

industryRisks.name: Pricing Inaccuracies
industryRisks.description: Dynamic pricing agent quotes wrong discounts due to tool schema mismatch.
industryRisks.example: Customer sees $99 laptop at $49 due to inventory tool parsing error.

industryRisks.name: Return Policy Misrepresentation
industryRisks.description: Memory bank hallucinates extended return windows not matching policy.
industryRisks.example: Agent promises 90-day returns when policy is 30 days, causing refund disputes.

industryRisks.name: PII Exposure in Order Confirmation
industryRisks.description: Safety shield false positive blocks legit order but memory leaks customer address.
industryRisks.example: Agent response includes full CC# in 'secure' order summary.

implementation.title: Verify Product Claims Against Catalog
implementation.description: Wrap Llama Stack agent response to validate claims match product metadata using Vex policy.

implementation.title: Enforce Pricing Schema Compliance
implementation.description: Validate pricing tool responses conform to expected JSON schema before customer display.

implementation.title: Block PII and PCI Data Leaks
implementation.description: Post-process agent memory and responses to redact sensitive data before persistence.
implementation.code:
from vex_sdk.pii import scan_pii

agent_memory = llama.get_memory('user_session_123')
agent_response = llama.invoke('Confirm my order #ORD-789')

# Scan for PII/PCI
pii_issues = scan_pii(agent_response + agent_memory)

result = verify(
    agent_response,
    policy='ecommerce-pii-pci',
    sensitivity='standard',
    redact=True
)

if result.valid:
    llama.set_memory('user_session_123', result.clean_response)
else:
    llama.respond('Order details redacted for security. Check email.')

caseStudyScenario.heading: Llama Stack in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
FashionRetailCo deployed Llama Stack agents for personalized shopping assistance, product recommendations, and checkout support. The agent used memory banks to track user preferences and tool calls to fetch real-time inventory/pricing. However, safety shields blocked 15% of legitimate queries as 'promotional spam,' memory retrieval hallucinated unavailable sizes, and pricing tools occasionally returned malformed JSON causing checkout failures. During Black Friday, customers received inconsistent refund policy information, leading to 3,000+ support tickets and FTC complaint inquiries about 'deceptive practices.'

The root causes traced to Llama Stack failure modes: false positive filtering, schema mismatches, and memory hallucinations. Without compliance guardrails, agents generated unsubstantiated 'best seller' claims and exposed PII in order summaries, risking GDPR fines and PCI violations.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex integration via vex.verify() post-processing reduced compliance incidents by 97%. Product claim verification against catalog data blocked all unsubstantiated statements. Pricing schema validation caught 100% of malformed tool responses before customer exposure. PII scanning redacted 2,345 order responses automatically. Customer churn dropped 23%, support tickets fell 68%, and Black Friday conversion rates increased 14% with trustworthy agent interactions.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and wrap your Llama Stack agents to eliminate compliance risks.
